hero.name: Armen Mkhitaryan
hero.title: From Chaos to System: Catalog, Product Cards, SEO, UI/UX, Standards
storytelling_description: Armenian e-commerce is often built around chaos rather than systems.
I’ve been inside that reality: unclear product pages, misplaced categories, weak SEO structure, repetitive tasks, and endless manual work.
Today, I help turn that chaos into a content operating system — clear, manageable, and focused on results.
Shall we turn the mess into a system?

about.title: Operational Content Leader
about.description: I build content systems where there is chaos: from categories, filters, SEO and product cards to standards, website visual logic, UI/UX connection and large-team coordination.
about.text_creation_desc: Categories, filters and product cards should work as one system.
about.content_management_desc: Systematic tasks, standards and team management turn chaos into a manageable process.
about.cms_desc: Content works when both UI/UX and graphic design work.
about.specialization_path: FROM CHAOTIC TASKS TO OPERATIONAL SYSTEM
about.help_understand: My path grew from product cards and SEO into catalog structure, website visual logic, standards, AI workflows and team leadership.
about.start_desc: Product cards, SEO text, categories, bulk SKU work and first quality standards.
about.growth_desc: Categorization, attributes, filters, CMS, marketplaces and content management.
about.senior_desc: Large-team coordination, standards, quality, AI automation and operational content development.
domus_responsibilities: categorization, filters, attributes and SEO logic
systematic solving of website and catalog problems
AI and automation of recurring workflows

skills.title: CONTENT SYSTEMS MANAGEMENT
skills.subtitle: Thinking and coordination
skills.principle: The focus is not on tools, but first on the ability to assemble a system: content audit, problem diagnosis, standards implementation, team development and content strengthening through connection with SEO, UI/UX and analytics tools.
skills.mentorship_desc: I do not teach people to write good texts. I provide paths and guides that help create and develop content through systemic thinking, avoiding demotivating chaos.
skills.video_calls_desc: Thinking and structure for getting out of chaos
skills.for_partnership_desc: Result does not come from more tasks. It comes from structure: right categories, clear standards, a strong team, quality control and content connected to user experience.

services.title: WHAT I SOLVE
services.subtitle: Solutions
services.description: My work is not texts, but noticing systemic problems in catalog and filtering, product cards, SEO, UI/UX, graphic design and the team, then turning them into a working process.

cooperation_benefits.discussion_desc: First, identify where the system breaks: catalog structure, filters, product cards, SEO, visuals, team or task flow.
cooperation_benefits.individual_desc: After diagnosis come rules: ownership, card standards, quality checks and team decision logic.
cooperation_benefits.transparent_desc: A system works only with regular checks: card QA, attributes, visual logic, SEO and user journey.

services.cta_description: Contact us and find out how we can help your business:

copywriting.title: Catalog without structure
copywriting.description: When categories, subcategories, attributes and filters do not help users find products.

blog.title: Team without standards
blog.description: When tasks are done differently, quality depends on individuals and ownership is unclear.

smm.title: Content and UI disconnected
smm.description: When copy, filters, page blocks and visuals live separately from the user scenario.

products.title: Product cards without system
products.description: When SEO, descriptions, specs, photos and visual blocks are not connected into one product logic.

presentations.title: Visuals without meaning
presentations.description: When graphic assets exist but do not explain the product, category or advantage.

audit.title: AI without process
audit.description: When AI is used chaotically, without rules, review, templates and ownership.

portfolio.title: PROJECTS
portfolio.subtitle: System approach
portfolio.description: Not a project list, but examples of areas where chaos becomes a managed process: catalog, team, SEO, filters, UI/UX and visuals.
portfolio.results_subtitle: Areas that require quality management, not one-off text writing.
portfolio.approach_kpi: Retain quality through QA, team and AI
portfolio.cta_description: Tell us about your project and we'll find the best solution together:
portfolio.case_ecommerce_type_value: 10,000 SKU in Armenian, English and Russian
portfolio.case_ecommerce_specialization_value: catalog, categorization, templates and content process

pricing.title: FORMATS
pricing.subtitle: I do not sell knowledge, I share experience
pricing.description: The format depends on the problem: diagnosis, mentorship or operational participation.
pricing.not_found_desc: Describe where the chaos is: catalog, team, filters, SEO, cards, UI/UX or visuals.
pricing.included_strategy: current chaos and root cause review
pricing.included_guarantee: focus on a result that can be retained

small.name: DIAGNOSIS
small.annual: audit of catalog, team and process
small.description: For situations where the site, catalog or team works chaotically and the cause is unclear.

large.name: MENTORSHIP
large.description: For a team that needs rules, ownership, checklists and systematic thinking.

project.name: OPERATIONS
project.annual: operational participation in small e-commerce projects
project.description: For small e-commerce projects that need coordination of content, website and workflow.

contact.title: CONTACT
contact.subtitle: For solving problems
contact.description: Write if you want to discuss content system, team, catalog, website or mentorship.
contact.message_placeholder: What is not working now: catalog, cards, team, SEO, filters, UI/UX, visuals or processes?
contact.success_message: Thanks for the message. I'll contact you within 1 business day.
contact.error_message: Error. Please try to contact me another way.
contact.quick_response_desc: I respond as quickly as possible. I prefer written communication, but if needed we can organize a video call within 3 days.

terms.title: Mentorship, consultation and content audit
terms.subtitle: Formats for teams and small e-commerce websites that need to turn chaos into a clear system.

content.0: For content managers and small teams that need standards, logic and systematic thinking.
content.1: We can work individually or with the team using real tasks and mistakes.
content.2: The focus is practical rules, checklists and working approaches.

services.title: 2. Consultation and audit

provided.0: Review of catalog, product cards, SEO, filtering, UI/UX, visuals and team workflows.

not_provided.0: I do not replace full-service marketing, development, targeting or Ads management.

services.combined_note: For small e-commerce websites, a short audit with a problem map and priorities is possible.

content.0: Timing depends on the format and scope.
content.1: For audits, pages, categories or workflows are agreed in advance.

content.0: Format and cost are agreed in advance.
The outcome can be a problem map, standard, checklist, guideline or action plan.
Information about the site, catalog, team and internal workflows is not published without permission.
content.1: NDA can be discussed if needed.

content.0: Mentorship requires team involvement to produce results.
content.1: Consultation gives direction; implementation remains with the business or team.
content.2: Content audit does not replace technical SEO or full-stack audit.

0.question: Who is mentorship for?
0.answer: For content managers, junior/middle specialists and small teams that need systematic work.

1.question: What is included in consultation?
1.answer: Review of a specific problem: catalog, cards, SEO, filters, visuals, process or AI.

2.question: What is an audit for small e-commerce?
2.answer: A quick system review of the website and catalog with a problem map and priorities.

3.question: Can it be team-based?
3.answer: Yes, team format is useful for standards, roles and ownership.

4.question: Do you teach AI tools?
4.answer: Yes, as part of the workflow: prompts, templates, acceleration and quality control.
